name: TAO 4h Deep Reset Partial Runner v1
edge_mechanism: a deeper pullback resets momentum, price reclaims fast structure, early profit is taken aggressively, and only a reduced core is left for continuation if the reset becomes a real second leg
thesis: The cycle wants a trend-reset idea, but earlier TAO reset families died from sparse all-or-nothing continuation logic. This version changes the management expression: monetize the first relief leg aggressively and treat persistence as optional upside rather than a requirement. TAO 4h is the best fit because it has the amplitude to make both early partials and occasional runners meaningful.
invalidation_condition: If deeper resets still trade too rarely, or if early partials fail to clear costs often enough, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price undergoes a deeper pullback, reclaims fast structure from within medium-term support, and produces at least a relief leg with some chance of continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: TAO carries some trend-up contamination but remains structurally noisy. A management-first expression is more appropriate than demanding every reset become a big continuation leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because the lane has enough amplitude that a smaller retained core can still matter after early profit capture, while the early partial structure helps survive noise.

indicator_role_map.ATR_14: required reset depth and stop sizing

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The deeper reset may still be actual trend failure, or TAO may only produce brief relief bounces that do not justify even partial continuation positioning.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets work but the runner consistently gives back gains, cut runner size further before changing entry conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Most expectancy should come from early partial profit, with a smaller contribution from occasional continuation runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h shows enough reset-reclaim events, first-target hit rate is credible, and the reduced core adds value without erasing gains.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or early partials that still cannot survive post-cost noise.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but more forgiving than earlier reset families because management no longer requires large continuation to validate the trade

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for deeper reset plus partial-profit runner logic.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough reset-reclaim events and early partials clear costs regularly.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h resets remain too sparse or too unstable even for a management-first expression.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with reliable first partials and occasional runner contribution.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same reset-management expression works on the cycle primary lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 4h preserves the same reset-reclaim-relief pattern with slightly smaller amplitude.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: SOL 4h removes the mechanism or makes it too flat.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, cleaner first-target behavior than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the management expression survives on a cleaner chop lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough relief-leg behavior after reset to validate portability.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the mechanism needs TAO amplitude to exist.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, cleaner but smaller relief legs than TAO.
